# Z46_MISSION_LIFECYCLE_REPORT.md
## Phase Z46D — Mission Lifecycle Realization Report

*Generated by Phase Z46 — nx-z46.js*

---

### Mission Lifecycle States

Z46D implements visual and behavioral support for all 7 mission lifecycle states:

| # | State | Trigger | Visual Treatment |
|---|-------|---------|-----------------|
| 1 | Creating | `session.start` / `session.started` | Brief blue flash on center panel |
| 2 | Running | `data-nx-exec="running"` | Activity bar visible, idle hero hidden |
| 3 | HITL / Waiting | `data-nx-state="hitl"` | Purple pulsing dot, HITL strip shown |
| 4 | Recovery | `data-nx-state="recovery"` | Amber accent on activity bar |
| 5 | Complete | `data-nx-state="complete"` | Green dot, completion summary |
| 6 | Replay | `data-nx-state="replay"` | Cyan accent (Z45C handles) |
| 7 | Failed | `data-nx-state="failed"` | Red dot + border on activity bar |

---

### State Transitions: Implementation Detail

#### State 1 — Creating

```css
body[data-nx-state="creating"] .nx-shell-center::after {
  /* Brief blue flash — 0.6s fade-out */
  animation: z46-creating-flash 0.6s ease-out forwards;
}
```
JS: `_onSessionStart()` fires on `session.start` / `session.started`, captures task text,
adds system message to chat panel.

#### State 2 — Running

```css
body[data-nx-exec="running"] #nxActivityBar { display: flex !important; }
body[data-nx-exec="running"] #nxIdleHero    { display: none; }
body[data-nx-exec="idle"]    #nxIdleHero    { display: flex; }
```
The toggle between idle hero and activity bar is driven by `data-nx-exec` (set by Z44).
Z46 adds no new logic here — only confirms the CSS is correct.

Activity bar shows:
- Animated dot (color reflects state)
- "AI is working…" text (updateable by Z44 narrative)
- File being written (`.nx-ab-file`)
- Write count badge
- Pause / Stop buttons (always functional)
- HITL wait indicator (purple, shown when `data-nx-state="hitl"`)
- Recovery progress indicator (amber, shown when `data-nx-state="recovery"`)
- Runtime warning (shown when Z37 risk = HIGH or CRITICAL)

#### State 3 — HITL / Waiting

```css
body[data-nx-state="hitl"] .nx-hitl-strip { display: block !important; }
```
Z46 adds a `.z46-hitl-wait` element in the activity bar:
```html
<div class="z46-hitl-wait">
  <span class="z46-hitl-wait-dot"></span> <!-- pulsing purple -->
  Awaiting operator approval
</div>
```
The HITL inject input in the left panel and the operator chat note input both funnel to the
same instruction channel.

#### State 4 — Recovery

```css
body[data-nx-state="recovery"] #nxActivityBar {
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(217,119,6,0.3);
}
body[data-nx-state="recovery"] .z46-recovery-progress { display: flex; }
```
Z46 adds a `.z46-recovery-progress` indicator: "↻ Recovery in progress…"
Chat panel receives: `_addChatMsg('agent', 'Attempting recovery…')` via `z36.recovery` event.

#### State 5 — Complete

```css
body[data-nx-state="complete"] .nx-ab-dot { background: #4CAF50 !important; }
body[data-nx-state="complete"] .z46-completion-summary { display: flex; }
```
`_onSessionDone()` fires on `session.done`:
- Updates `body[data-nx-state]`
- Adds "Mission completed." to chat panel
- Triggers file panel refresh (if open)
- Refreshes history panel (if open)
- Updates last mission card in idle hero
- Refreshes readiness strip

#### State 6 — Replay

Handled by Z45C (cyan palette, mission bar narrative, cursor sync).
Z46D ensures the activity bar shows the replay state via `body[data-nx-state="replay"]`.

#### State 7 — Failed

```css
body[data-nx-state="failed"] #nxActivityBar {
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(192,57,43,0.3);
}
body[data-nx-state="failed"] .nx-ab-dot { background: #F87171 !important; }
```
Chat panel receives "Mission failed." via `_onSessionDone`.

---

### Lifecycle Chat Narrative

The chat panel now captures lifecycle events automatically:

| Event | Chat message |
|-------|-------------|
| Session start (with prompt) | `system: Mission started: [prompt]` |
| Prompt submit | `operator: [prompt text]` |
| Agent message | `agent: [message]` |
| HITL pause | `system: Execution paused — awaiting operator input.` |
| Recovery | `agent: Attempting recovery…` |
| Session complete | `system: Mission completed.` |
| Session failed | `system: Mission failed.` |

---

### State Visibility Summary

```
State        Activity Bar  Idle Hero  HITL Strip  Recovery   Warning
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
idle         hidden        visible    hidden       hidden     hidden
creating     hidden        visible    hidden       hidden     hidden
running      visible       hidden     hidden       hidden     if risk HIGH
hitl         visible       hidden     visible      hidden     hidden
recovery     visible+amber hidden     hidden       visible    hidden
complete     visible+green hidden     hidden       hidden     hidden
failed       visible+red   hidden     hidden       hidden     hidden
replay       visible+cyan  hidden     hidden       hidden     hidden
```

---

### Remaining Lifecycle Gaps

1. **State "creating" duration**: The brief flash is cosmetic only — no distinct creating state in the backend. Session moves directly from idle to running.

2. **Completion summary panel**: `.z46-completion-summary` CSS class is defined and shown in `body[data-nx-state="complete"]`, but a rich completion card (showing tokens used, time taken, files written) would require session summary data from the backend.

3. **HITL approval queue**: The `z33ApprovalsRow` element shows pending approvals from NxBus but only when the Z33 module populates it. Z46 does not add new approval UI — the existing HITL strip is the approval mechanism.

4. **State persistence across reload**: If the user reloads during a running session, the state resets to idle. Session recovery via SSE auto-reconnect handles this (existing feature).

---

### Production Readiness Verdict

> **PASS** — All 7 lifecycle states have distinct visual treatments. State transitions are CSS-driven using existing `data-nx-state` and `data-nx-exec` attributes (set by Z44). The activity bar shows contextually appropriate indicators for each state. The chat panel captures the full lifecycle narrative automatically.
